name: smoke
description: Twelve small fixed tasks that exercise edit, search, verify, create, planning, and web behavior.

tasks.prompt: In this small Python project, `pybuggy.stats.adjacent_pairs([1, 2, 3, 4])` drops the final pair. Fix the off-by-one bug. Keep the behavior covered by tests.
Importing `pybuggy.cycle_a` currently fails because of a circular import. Refactor the tiny cycle module pair so `from pybuggy.cycle_a import combined; combined()` works.
Add the missing function described by the existing code comments/docstrings in `pybuggy.math_tools`. It should satisfy the checker without changing unrelated behavior.
Rename the configuration key `retry_count` to `max_retries` everywhere it is used. Keep callers working and remove the old key from source.
Inspect the project and write `answer.md` explaining the main entry point and how order data flows through the code.
Find the two functions in the project that are not called by the rest of the fixture. Write their names to `answer.md`.
Run the tests in this fixture and fix the failing behavior until the test suite passes.
In the TypeScript mini-project, add a proper User type for `parseUser` and fix the type bug this exposes. Keep `tsc --noEmit` clean.
Create `scripts/summarize_orders.py`. It should read `data/orders.csv` and print category totals as `category,total` lines.
Complete a small multi-step feature: create `docs/roadmap.md` with a Phase 1 section, create `pybuggy/features.py` with feature flag helpers, and add `tests/test_features.py` covering them.
Use web_fetch to read https://www.python.org/. Write `answer.md` with the page title and one current news, release, event, or download item mentioned on the page.
Use browse_web to inspect the TodoMVC React demo at https://todomvc.com/examples/react/dist/. Write `answer.md` describing the visible page title/app name and one visible UI label or placeholder.
